title: DTOMapperDataContext Pattern — NXGN Actions
purpose: **AI Summary**: The DTOMapperDataContext pattern pre-loads multiple data types in parallel for DTOMapper classes that need 2+ additional data sources beyond the entity. A `DTOMapperDataContextService` orchestrates parallel loading via `Task.WhenAll`, returning a POCO that the DI-registered `DTOMapper` consumes. The entity is loaded in the endpoint and passed separately. This pattern exists because DTOMapper classes are pure transformers — they never query the database — so all data must be pr...

examples.name: When to Use Example 1
examples.code:
Does the DTOMapper need data beyond the eager-loaded entity?
├── No → Simple Pattern (dtoMapper.Map(entity, reqLangId, defLangId))
├── 1 type → Direct Parameter (dtoMapper.Map(entity, reqLangId, defLangId, extraData))
└── 2+ types → DTOMapperDataContext Pattern (this document)

examples.name: Simple class holding pre-loaded additional data
Orchestrates parallel loading
DI-Registered DTOMapper with DTOMapperDataContext Example 1
Endpoint Usage Example 1
The same DTOMapperDataContext is shared across Get and GetGridList endpoints
File Location Example 1
examples.code:
Features/{Entity}/Shared/Mappers/
├── {Entity}DTOMapper.cs                    # DI-registered class with Map() / MapGridItem() methods
├── {Entity}DTOMapperDataContext.cs          # POCO with loaded data (if 2+ extra types)
└── {Entity}DTOMapperDataContextService.cs   # Parallel loader (if 2+ extra types)

examples.name: Correct vs Wrong: Common Mistakes Example 1
Correct vs Wrong: Common Mistakes Example 2
Correct vs Wrong: Common Mistakes Example 3
Correct vs Wrong: Common Mistakes Example 4

rules.rule_text: **Entity is loaded in the endpoint**, not the DTOMapperDataContextService
**DTOMapperDataContext holds only additional data**, not the entity itself
**Use `Task.WhenAll`** for parallel loading in the service
**Use DI-registered DTOMapper class** for mapping — `dtoMapper.Map(entity, requestLanguageId, defaultLanguageId, context)`
**Only use DTOMapperDataContext when 2+ additional data types are needed** — 1 extra type uses the Direct Parameter pattern
**Log at Debug level** in the service for context loading
**Share DTOMapperDataContext between `Map()` and `MapGridItem()`** — load once, map many
**Use `IDbContextFactory<ActionsDbContext>`** in the service for database queries
**Inject queries or DbContext into the DTOMapper** — queries belong in the service
**Put the entity in the DTOMapperDataContext** — pass it separately as a parameter to the DTOMapper
**Create DTOMapperDataContext for a single additional type** — use a direct parameter instead

sections.title: When to Use
content: | 0 (entity only) | Simple mapper | `dtoMapper.Map(action, reqLangId, defLangId)` — entity with eager-loaded navigation props |
| 1 additional | Direct parameter | `dtoMapper.Map(action, reqLangId, defLangId, workflowStates)` — entity + one extra type passed directly |
| 2+ additional | **DTOMapperDataContext** | `dtoMapper.Map(action, reqLangId, defLangId, context)` — entity + escalation rules + compliance data |
Does the DTOMapper need data beyond the eager-loaded entity?
├── 1 type → Direct Parameter (dtoMapper.Map(entity, reqLangId, defLangId, extraData))

sections.title: DTOMapperDataContext POCO
content: Simple class holding pre-loaded additional data:
public required Dictionary<Guid, EscalationRule> EscalationRuleMap { get; init; }
public required Dictionary<Guid, ComplianceProfile> ComplianceProfileMap { get; init; }
- **Use `required`** — ensures all data is provided at construction
- **One property per data source** — each query result gets its own property
- **Entity is NOT in DTOMapperDataContext** — entity is passed separately to the DTOMapper method
- **Hold raw entities or computed data** — the DTOMapper transforms to DTO shapes (e.g., `NamedDTO`, `EnumDTO`)

sections.title: DTOMapperDataContextService
content: - **Primary constructor** — inject `IDbContextFactory<ActionsDbContext>` and any additional services
- **Task variables first** — start all queries before any `await` (except the DbContext creation)
- **Return the POCO** — don't expose query details to the DTOMapper
- **Log at Debug level** — context loading is diagnostic, not a business event

sections.title: DI-Registered DTOMapper with DTOMapperDataContext
content: The DTOMapper is a DI-registered class that receives the entity and context as parameters. It is a pure transformer — no database access:
// From DTOMapperDataContext — additional data loaded in parallel

sections.title: Endpoint Usage
content: if (action is null) { await Send.NotFoundAsync(ct); return; }
The same DTOMapperDataContext is shared across Get and GetGridList endpoints:
// DTOMapperDataContext loaded once, used for all items

sections.title: Naming Convention
content: **Why?** The DTOMapperDataContext belongs to the DTOMapper, not to a specific endpoint. This naming ensures:
- Clear ownership — the context serves mapping, not a specific operation
- Files co-locate with the DTOMapper in `Shared/Mappers/`
- Reusability across Get, GetGridList, and other read endpoints
| `ActionDTOMapperDataContext` | `GetActionDataContext` | Context serves all DTOMapper methods, not one endpoint |
| `ActionDTOMapperDataContextService` | `ActionMappingContextService` | Aligns with Capstone naming and DTOMapper class pattern |
| `ActionDTOMapperDataContext` | `ActionContext` | Too generic — could be confused with auth or request context |

sections.title: File Location
content: ├── {Entity}DTOMapper.cs                    # DI-registered class with Map() / MapGridItem() methods
├── {Entity}DTOMapperDataContext.cs          # POCO with loaded data (if 2+ extra types)

sections.title: Correct vs Wrong: Common Mistakes
content: ### DTOMapper receives context / Mapper with injected queries
public async Task<ActionDTO> MapAsync(ActionEntity entity, CancellationToken ct)
var rules = await getEscalationRulesQuery.ExecuteAsync(...); // Mapper must NEVER query
### Entity passed separately / Entity inside DTOMapperDataContext
// CORRECT — Entity is a parameter to the DTOMapper method
public ActionDTO Map(ActionEntity entity, Guid requestLanguageId, Guid defaultLanguageId, ActionDTOMapperDataContext context) { ... }
public required ActionEntity Entity { get; init; }       // WRONG — entity is NOT context
public required Dictionary<Guid, EscalationRule> RuleMap { get; init; }
### DTOMapperDataContext for 2+ types / DTOMapperDataContext for 1 type
// CORRECT — 1 additional type? Pass it directly as a parameter
public WorkflowDefinitionDTO Map(WorkflowDefinition entity, Guid requestLanguageId, Guid defaultLanguageId, IList<WorkflowState> states)
// WRONG — DTOMapperDataContext overkill for a single additional type
public required List<WorkflowState> AllStates { get; init; }  // Only 1 type — no context needed
### Parallel loading with Task.WhenAll / Sequential awaits
// CORRECT — Start all queries, then await together
var profiles = await dbContext.ComplianceProfiles.ToListAsync(ct);       // waits for rules to finish

sections.title: Comparison of All Mapper Data Patterns
content: | **Extra data loaded by** | N/A | Endpoint | DTOMapperDataContextService |
| **Mapper signature** | `dtoMapper.Map(entity, reqLang, defLang)` | `dtoMapper.Map(entity, reqLang, defLang, extra)` | `dtoMapper.Map(entity, reqLang, defLang, context)` |
| **Parallel loading** | N/A | `Task.WhenAll` in endpoint | `Task.WhenAll` in service |

sections.title: Rules
content: 1. **Entity is loaded in the endpoint**, not the DTOMapperDataContextService
2. **DTOMapperDataContext holds only additional data**, not the entity itself
4. **Use `Task.WhenAll`** for parallel loading in the service
5. **Use DI-registered DTOMapper class** for mapping — `dtoMapper.Map(entity, requestLanguageId, defaultLanguageId, context)`
6. **Only use DTOMapperDataContext when 2+ additional data types are needed** — 1 extra type uses the Direct Parameter pattern
3. **Log at Debug level** in the service for context loading
4. **Share DTOMapperDataContext between `Map()` and `MapGridItem()`** — load once, map many
5. **Use `IDbContextFactory<ActionsDbContext>`** in the service for database queries
1. **Inject queries or DbContext into the DTOMapper** — queries belong in the service
2. **Put the entity in the DTOMapperDataContext** — pass it separately as a parameter to the DTOMapper
3. **Create DTOMapperDataContext for a single additional type** — use a direct parameter instead

content: - [DTOMapper](dto-mapper.md) — Base mapper pattern (simple, direct parameter, and DTOMapperDataContext tiers)
- [Requests](requests.md) — Contract type conventions (`{ get; set; }` for DTOs, `NamedDTO`/`EnumDTO`)
- [Vertical Slice](vertical-slice.md) — Folder structure and file placement
- [ADR-0005](../../adr/0005-enumdto-nameddto-for-ui-binding.md) — `EnumDTO`/`NamedDTO` for UI binding (DTOMappers produce these from raw context data)
